news.title: 1 person hospitalized with serious injuries after shooting in Decatur, police say
news.summary: DeKalb County Police responded to the scene located at 3490 Blazing Pine Path and located a person who had been shot.

news.title: Grandmother charged in drowning death of 2-year-old at Roswell apartments, warrants reveal
news.summary: Warrants state that the grandmother's neglect ultimately resulted in Delgado's death.

news.title: Former Acworth official charged in bond theft case
news.summary: Former Acworth court administrator Trevez Richards is jailed in Cobb County, accused of stealing over half a million dollars in bond money.

news.title: Louisiana man accused of two I-85 road rage shootings in less than 24 hours in Troup County
news.summary: “You never know what kind of day that other person is having that cut you off and you blow the horn, or you give them the finger.”

news.title: Brookhaven business owner, massage therapist arrested after investigation, police say
news.summary: Brookhaven Police officers responded to a complaint at the Balance & Relax Massage, located at 2484 Briarcliff Road.

news.title: Boyfriend of mother found dead in Atlanta home in 1999 cold case charged with murder
news.summary: An autopsy was completed in 1999, but those results were never communicated to the Atlanta Police Homicide Unit, leaving the case unsolved until now.

news.title: Driver shot in road rage incident on southbound I-85 in Hogansville
news.summary: A motorist dodged a hail of gunfire on the interstate before leading police directly to the shooter during a high-stakes pursuit across Georgia county lines.

news.title: Employee at Smyrna mental health facility arrested for raping underage patient, warrants reveal
news.summary: ​The assault was reported to the facility on April 16, and an internal investigation began.

news.title: Men charged federally with threatening Jewish employees at Atlanta-based Flock Safety, PrizePicks
news.summary: The arrest marks the fourth person in 12 months to be federally charged in the Northern District of Georgia, including two Georgia men.
